item.title: NSW shark-spotting drone program expanded after Coogee Beach shark attack
item.description: Shark-spotting drones will provide year-round coverage at about 70 beaches along the NSW coast as part of an expanded surveillance program announced after a Sydney mother was attacked by a suspected white shark.

item.title: Curtin University program helps connect young autistic people to cyber security industry
item.description: The cybersecurity sector is projected to need tens of thousands of roles filled by 2030. Now, a university-based program is helping provide those workers by tapping into the abilities of autistic people.

item.title: Wallabies and All Blacks to play Bledisloe Cup Tests on Anzac Day at Lang Park
item.description: For the first time in 123 years, the Wallabies and All Blacks will play a Bledisloe Cup Test on Anzac Day, with Lang Park hosting in 2027, 2029 and 2031.

item.title: Long-awaited Perth-to-Bunbury train service reopens
item.description: The disruption-plagued Perth-to-Bunbury train service is back on track following years of breakdowns and delays, but locals remain frustrated over the rollout.

item.title: Beyond the Shed training program prepares WA shearers for their future and a stronger local workforce
item.description: Almost 100 shearing shed workers across regional WA sign up to a new training program aimed at helping contractors and their teams find work beyond the shearing industry.

item.title: Thai teenager's family grieves as Australian charged with murder says death was 'out of my control'
item.description: The family of a 17-year-old girl in Thailand speak of their grief as an Australian man is charged with her murder.

item.title: Love story: Holding onto the glimmers of my dad, Blues legend Steve Mortimer
item.description: Erin Mortimer's dad, Bulldogs and Blues legend Steve Mortimer, was diagnosed with dementia at just 60 after years of concussions during his playing career. Erin is holding on to the special memories, and the glimmers of him that remain.

item.title: Bali's beauty sinks beneath an 'Island of Trash' as waste issues increase
item.description: Known to many as the Island of the Gods, residents fear Bali may soon become the "Island of Trash" due to a deluge of tourists, a lack of rubbish education and the partial closure of a much-needed landfill facility.

item.title: Treasurer Jim Chalmers promises to 'fix' property tax oversight to protect widows and divorcees
item.description: The Treasurer is promising to "fix" an unintended consequence of the government's overhaul of property tax breaks to protect widows and divorcees — but he has refused to say exactly how.

item.title: New parents struggling with anxiety and depression urged to seek free counselling services
item.description: New parents often struggle in silence, but a growing number of perinatal clinics offering free mental health support are being rolled out across Australia.

item.title: Man charged after four-month-old baby's death in 2024
item.description: The 22-year-old Bundaberg man has been charged with domestic violence offences of murder and torture.

item.title: Young station ringers win Battle of the Barkly at the NT outback's Brunette Races
item.description: Based on the day in the life of an outback station ringer, the Battle of the Barkly obstacle course has been won by two young cattle industry workers.

item.title: Blues ring in the changes as To'o dumped for decider
item.description: New South Wales coach Laurie Daley has made five changes to the Blues side for Origin III with Brian To'o missing out for the first time in over five years.

item.title: Face paint, chants and flags — the many ways football fans celebrate the World Cup
item.description: From donning face paint to viral chants, each fandom has its peculiarities.

item.title: Jackson the star as Dockers make it 14 wins in a row
item.description: The Fremantle Dockers keep rolling along, winning their 14th straight game with a dominant victory over Gold Coast after the Kangaroos beat Essendon. Follow live.

item.title: Father of Thai girl found in suitcase shares grief
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Australian run of Waitress closing early with Sydney shows cancelled
item.description: The Australian stage production of Waitress will no longer continue on its national tour, with planned shows in Sydney now cancelled.

item.title: England captain Ben Stokes to retire from international cricket after third Test vs New Zealand
item.description: England captain Ben Stokes will retire from international cricket after the third Test against New Zealand, the team has said.

item.title: Race Around The World recap: Catch the week four films
item.description: From survival vaults in Norway to a pianist in Pisa, catch up on the films from week four of Race Around The World.

item.title: This week in history quiz June 29: Test your smarts on Eminem, Jane Austen and competitive chess
item.description: This week was a big one in times gone by. From this decade to the 17th century, put your history knowledge to the test.

item.title: Do you qualify for three hours of free electricity? Here's how it works
item.description: Under the federal government's Solar Sharer scheme, some Australian households will be able to access three hours of free electricity a day. Here's how you can make the most of it.

item.title: Gulf states 'colder, harder and more transactional' under US-Iran deal
item.description: Before a flare-up in fighting over the weekend, the interim deal between the United States and Iran was giving the region something it badly needs, analysts say.

item.title: Trump allies in the South are wiping out Black votes
item.description: States in America's South are scrambling to draw up new electoral maps that would eliminate majority-Black electorates.

item.title: Iran and China are simultaneously driving a major energy transition
item.description: Two things happened last week that give us a much clearer picture of what the world is going to be like from now on.

item.title: As Taylor struggles to handle Hanson, Hastie talks tough on One Nation
item.description: While Angus Taylor grapples with the Coalition's dilemma of how to keep the centre while diverting voters away from Pauline Hanson's party, Andrew Hastie has been talking tough on One Nation.

item.title: Dozens of Australian Rules players diagnosed with CTE expose scale of AFL's brain injury crisis
item.description: Australian Rules footballers who never played in the game's premier league are being diagnosed with CTE, revealing that the brain trauma crisis striking all levels of the game.

item.title: Coal and gas giants spending tens of millions on programs in schools and children's sport
item.description: Santos, BHP, Woodside and others have an "extensive presence" in childhood settings through more than 260 programs, a new report identifies, as senators say classroom programs and lessons written by the industry should be examined through a senate inquiry.

item.title: Lawyers band together as Tasmania's court backlog hits 'crisis point'
item.description: Routine cases are dragging through Tasmanian courts for years as senior lawyers lament the "crisis" engulfing the state's criminal justice system.

item.title: Vanuatu critical infrastructure to remain 'free from militarisation' under Nakamal pact
item.description: Vanuatu's Prime Minister Jotham Napat and Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese ink the Nakamal Agreement, almost 10 months after Mr Napat pulled out of a planned signing ceremony in Port Vila, citing concerns around sovereignty.

item.title: The national program teaching boys as well as girls about endometriosis, periods and pelvic pain
item.description: Educators behind a national program teaching high school students about periods and pelvic pain hope it will encourage empathy.

item.title: Man charged with DV murder after woman shot on the Gold Coast
item.description: Ethan Midgely, 23, faces one domestic violence murder charge after he allegedly shot and killed who police believe was his de facto partner.

item.title: Customers in remote Australia have safety concerns as Telstra phases out CAN Radio phone system
item.description: Telstra's Customer Access Network Radio has been a lifeline for many for more than 40 years but its technology has reached the end of its life. Customers now fear a new system will not be as reliable in an emergency.

item.title: Venezuelan earthquakes rescuers save infant and two boys alive from rubble
item.description: The three children are among at least 33 people rescued during a weekend multinational operation that continued searching for survivors, while thousands remained unaccounted-for.

item.title: Tech revival sees ASX close 0.7pc higher, Neuren leaps on EU approval hopes — as it happened
item.description: The ASX shrugged off a sluggish Wall Street lead to close 0.7 per cent higher as Neuren Pharmaceuticals surged on a potential EU approval for one of its drugs.

item.title: Ellyse Perry and Ash Gardner carry Australia to T20 World Cup win over India
item.description: A powerful partnership by Ellyse Perry and Ash Gardner carries Australia to the greatest run chase in Women's T20 World Cup history, knocking India out of the tournament.

item.title: NT Police says new public safety officers on the beat from today will receive the same firearms training as constables
item.description: The Northern Territory Police Force has moved to reassure Territorians that new, armed public safety officers due to hit the beat from today are fully trained to carry guns.

item.title: Mt Rawdon pumped hydro project shelved by Qld government
item.description: A $6 billion dollar project to rehabilitate a gold mine and turn it into a pumped hydro power station has been dumped by the Queensland government.

item.title: Maroons recall veteran for Origin decider after Blues' bombshell
item.description: Billy Slater names Patrick Carrigan in his Queensland squad for State of Origin III despite the Broncos forward having not played since late May due to an ankle injury.

item.title: Farmer's push to return lost CSIRO archives reveals 200 years of merino history
item.description: More than 1,000 kilograms of wool samples, lab notes and technical drawings spent decades gathering dust in a British home. A Victorian sheep farmer just brought them back.

item.title: Socceroos to play Brazil at Lang Park in first home game after World Cup
item.description: The first of the two friendlies will be at Brisbane's Lang Park on September 25, and the second at Townsville on September 29.

item.title: France records 1,000 excess daily deaths as Europe's record heatwave sparks power outages and wildfires
item.description: More than 150 million Europeans swelter in "extreme heat", which scientists say has been the worst the continent has ever recorded.

item.title: Spectacular lightning display at the Eiffel Tower
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: World Cup: Has the chaotic, but engaging group stage justified the expanded tournament?
item.description: Ghana's coach Carlos Queiroz has lashed out at FIFA's expanded 48-team World Cup, despite his team progressing. Is his criticism justified as the tournament moves into the knockout stage?

item.title: Ben Stokes retirement highlights his main character energy was not always a force for good
item.description: Ben Stokes has been the centre of England's cricketing universe for 15 years. In announcing his retirement mid-match, he highlighted how damaging that main character energy can be.

item.title: Artist's impression of NASA's Swift Boost Mission
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: NASA Swift rescue mission spacecraft will bump telescope back into orbit
item.description: US space agency NASA is racing to save an aging telescope, used to observe cosmic explosions, from falling back to Earth with a daring rescue mission.

item.title: Crows star Chelsea Randall says concussion left with her 'no choice' but to retire from AFLW
item.description: Adelaide Crows star Chelsea Randall announces her retirement from the AFLW, citing concussion as a major factor in her decision to hang up her boots and step away from the game.

item.title: WA regional courts to reopen a month earlier than planned after security issues
item.description: Regional West Australian courts closed amid a stoush over security services are set to reopen about a month earlier than originally planned.

item.title: Canberra man found guilty of attempted murder after stabbing his wife in violent home attack
item.description: An ACT Supreme Court jury has found a Canberra man guilty of attempted murder after he stabbed his wife during a violent attack in the family's Gungahlin home.

item.title: Serena Williams criticises tennis anti-doping protocol ahead of Wimbledon return
item.description: As she prepares for her All England Club comeback, Serena Williams claims the tennis anti-doping protocol is "unprofessional" and "unreasonable".

item.title: Winton's film festival kicks off as Red Dog director reveals new thriller
item.description: Red Dog director Kriv Stenders reveals plans for a new thriller film as the annual Vision Splendid Outback Film Festival kicks off in Winton.

item.title: Les Mills, NZ Olympian and founder of international fitness brand, dies aged 91
item.description: The New Zealand Olympian who opened an Auckland gym in 1968 that grew into an international group fitness brand has died aged 91.

item.title: Father charged with murder and torture of four-month-old baby to remain in custody
item.description: Police say the baby's condition was consistent with "a child-shaking injury".

item.title: Outback roads badly damaged by flooding could take year to fix
item.description: Damage to South Australia's network of outback roads is isolating remote communities, preventing children from getting to school, and slowing the restocking process for pastoralists recovering from drought.

item.title: Family who spent $250,000 on IVF welcomes new rebate scheme in SA
item.description: A South Australian couple who spent $250,000 on fertility treatments say the government's IVF rebate will make a "huge difference" for people trying to conceive.

item.title: Health minister declines to request coronial inquest into baby Willow Horne's death in Kalgoorlie
item.description: WA's health minister says she would support a coronial inquest into the death of an eight-month-old girl at Kalgoorlie Health Campus, but says it is a decision for the state coroner.

item.title: Superannuation switching trend sees billions flow out of retail funds, at a risk
item.description: There is now more than $1 trillion in self-managed superannuation funds, which means a big chunk of money isn't regulated by APRA, and it's the rate of the shift into SMSFs and less-regulated managed investment schemes that has regulators worried.

item.title: Calls for SA drivers to slow down and pay attention amid spate of fatal crashes
item.description: Police are calling on drivers to stay attentive and slow down after four people were killed on one region's roads in just 12 days.

item.title: Broncos winger Josiah Karapani stood down after alleged drink driving, traffic offences
item.description: In a statement, the club confirmed the 24-year-old had been issued with a notice to appear in court and would not be available for the next NRL game.

item.title: Four quick and easy dinners using inexpensive chickpeas
item.description: From comforting curry to hearty soup, these easy recipes will get chickpeas out of the pantry and onto the menu.

item.title: Blues coach Laurie Daley to answer critics after State of Origin III against Maroons
item.description: Laurie Daley says he will address criticism about his performance as Blues coach once the State of Origin series is over.

item.title: Alleged Russian spies Kira and Igor Korolev charged with new espionage offence
item.description: Crown prosecutors presented a new bench charge for the couple of conspiracy to commit espionage.

item.title: Queensland state electorates of Stretton and Hill to be abolished after review
item.description: Significant changes were made to 50 of Queensland's 93 electorates by the independent Queensland Redistribution Commission (QRC).

item.title: Brother tells of family's 'unimaginable pain' after doctor's death in Tasmania
item.description: The brother of Royal Hobart Hospital doctor Artyom Avetisyan says the cardiothoracic surgery registrar sounded "extremely exhausted" in the week before his crash death.

item.title: Bruce Lehrmann loses legal bid for Brittany Higgins documentary footage
item.description: Former Liberal staffer Bruce Lehrmann has been knocked back in a bid to gain access to raw documentary footage which featured Brittany Higgins.

item.title: Alleged victim of William 'Little Pebble' Kamm tells court of fear of escaping cult
item.description: An alleged victim of cult leader William "Little Pebble" Kamm has told a court she began experiencing extreme anxiety as she pondered an escape from a property on the NSW South Coast.

item.title: Billy Slater reveals stance on Maroons coaching future ahead of State of Origin III
item.description: Billy Slater says he will not sign another contract to be Queensland State of Origin coach, but that does not mean he will not continue in the role.

item.title: Network 10 agrees to temporary deal with WIN Television in country SA and NSW
item.description: Two regions in South Australia and one in New South Wales were set to lose access to free-to-air Network 10 services in coming days, but a new agreement means coverage will continue until the end of September.

item.title: WA Police stumped by apparent heist of 8 tonnes of tugboat propellers
item.description: Thieves have allegedly made away from a WA mining town with 8 tonnes of tugboat propellers, with the copper estimated to be worth an eye-watering $2 million.

item.title: ICAC hears UOW executive was dismissed the day he rejected colleague's pay rise
item.description: The ICAC inquiry has heard a former University of Wollongong executive was dismissed on the day of a disputed salary increase.

item.title: Monday finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Police union calls for tougher bail laws for repeat offenders after string of stolen car pursuits
item.description: A 14-year-old boy charged twice in three days after allegedly leading police on stolen car pursuits is among several incidents prompting the federal police union to call for tougher bail laws in the ACT.

item.title: Vladimir Putin says Russia will press on with frontline campaign despite fuel shortages
item.description: President Vladimir Putin has said Russia will press ahead with its battlefield aim of fully capturing four Ukrainian regions, rejecting diplomatic overtures from Ukraine.

item.title: Western Sydney International Airport prepares for take-off
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Alice Springs parole worker who helped fugitive killer evade authorities avoids jail time
item.description: An Alice Springs parole worker found in a hotel room with the 20-year-old she was responsible for supporting has avoided prison for attempting to pervert the course of justice.

item.title: To the Manor Born star Dame Penelope Keith dead at 86
item.description: Beloved British actress Dame Penelope Keith, who rose to fame in the hit 1970s UK sitcom The Good Life, has died aged 86.

item.title: PM-in-waiting Andy Burnham lays out plan to fix 'broken' British politics
item.description: Andy Burnham, the British MP tipped to replace the outgoing prime minister Sir Keir Starmer, promised "radical change" to the nation's "broken" political system as he laid out his vision to lead the country on Monday.

item.title: Sam Kerr's future confirmed as she signs for Gotham FC
item.description: Matildas striker Sam Kerr has returned to the United States following her successful stint at London giants, Chelsea.

item.title: Morocco advances after epic penalty shootout win over Netherlands
item.description: Morocco stays alive in the World Cup after defeating the Netherlands in a pulsating sudden-death penalty shootout.

item.title: Flag quiz, part 4: Will you raise the white flag on these tricky questions?
item.description: How many stars are there on the US flag? And how many stars are on the Europe flag?

item.title: Lodging too early and other mistakes people make when doing their taxes
item.description: With side hustles, working from home, crypto investments, renting out rooms and receiving income through digital platforms or apps, experts say people are at greater risk of making errors with their tax.

item.title: Ariane Beeston critiques ballet's toxic culture in new YA novel, Don't Forget to Smile
item.description: Ariane Beeston, the author of an acclaimed memoir detailing her experience with postpartum psychosis, has penned her first work of fiction, Don't Forget to Smile.

item.title: 'Systemic racism' claims identified in buried reef authority review
item.description: The damning results of a review into the agency tasked with managing the Great Barrier Reef, an area bigger than New Zealand and home to 70 traditional owner groups, has never before seen the light of day.

item.title: CTE poses a massive dilemma for the AFL. Experts say urgent action is needed to protect players
item.description: Contact is an essential part of footy, but there is growing evidence it can also be a grave threat to the health and welfare of its players. Experts say the AFL should be acting now to protect current and future players.

item.title: Government vows crackdown on sale of NDIS shell companies amid new registration rules
item.description: The federal government says it is moving to crack down on the growing trade in "off-the-shelf" NDIS providers.

item.title: Private health group Ramsay given special access to public hospital emergency patients
item.description: A trial to ease pressure on a public emergency department has raised concerns a multi-million-dollar company is getting a special deal.

item.title: US Supreme Court rejects Donald Trump's final appeal against E Jean Carroll verdict
item.description: The US Supreme Court declined to hear US President Donald Trump's bid to overturn a $5 million USD verdict in a case in which a jury found him liable for sexually abusing and defaming E Jean Carroll.

item.title: Are the wheels falling off the AI investment boom?
item.description: Overshadowing the tumult that is global politics has been a financial boom beyond almost anything witnessed before, focused entirely on the perceived benefits wrought by a brave new world of AI that threatens to up-end our lives.

item.title: ABC Overnights presenter Trevor Chappell announces his retirement after more than a quarter of a century
item.description: After decades of keeping night owls and shift workers company on ABC radio across Australia, Overnights presenter Trevor Chappell decides to call it a day at the age of 65.

item.title: Worker says Victoria's forestry transition program 'decimated' Gippsland's small towns
item.description: The Victorian Forestry Transition Program is ending. In the regions that relied on the timber trade, the question is whether it worked.

item.title: Simpson Desert in bloom but Birdsville's tourism season in limbo
item.description: Birdsville, a pilgrimage point for outback travellers, navigates the ripple effects of flood, fuel fears and the cancellation of a massive music festival, when the surrounding country has rarely looked so green and alive.

item.title: Midwife hit with $70,000 foreign investment tax after moving to Australia from UK
item.description: A British midwife who migrated to Australia to work at a regional hospital during statewide staff shortages is calling for changes to the law after she was slugged $70,000 by state and federal authorities for the purchase of her home.

item.title: Strait of Hormuz traffic was increasing until a fresh wave of strikes
item.description: Fresh attacks on commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz have exposed the fragility of the 60-day ceasefire deal between the US and Iran, which was meant to allow ships to transit through the waterway again.

item.title: Toll and public transport relief among new measures in NSW from July 1
item.description: Motorists, public transport users and wage earners are the biggest winners under new changes across NSW starting from July 1.

item.title: Seven Australians in Venezuela request help as earthquake death toll rises
item.description: Australia is providing "consular assistance" to seven people after Venezuela's two earthquakes, Foreign Affairs Minister Penny Wong confirms.

item.title: Coroner rules hiker's death in Mount Canobolas bushland not suspicious, praises 12-day search
item.description: The NSW coroner praises emergency services for "persistence" during the 12-day search for a hiker who set off on an ill-fated midnight mountain walk in sub-zero temperatures while wearing sandals.

item.title: Daria Kasatkina wins Wimbledon opener against local wildcard Mimi Xu
item.description: After a winless grass-court swing, Australian Daria Kasatkina becomes Australia's first winner at Wimbledon for 2026.

item.title: Tourism operator wants more information on recognising bird flu amid sick bird sightings
item.description: A tourism operator on South Australia's coast is frustrated by what he says is poor communication from authorities about bird flu. He says he saw sick birds weeks ago but did not know to report the sighting.

item.title: Jannik Sinner survives Wimbledon scare, Aryna Sabalenka coasts, Naomi Osaka rocks kimono
item.description: Jannik Sinner is pushed to the brink and Aryna Sabalenka coasts into the second round as both top seeds bounce back from disappointment at the French Open.

item.title: World Cup: How a punt on a trial in Italy turned Cristian Volpato into the next Socceroos star
item.description: Cristian Volpato was rejected by two Australian soccer academies. His family dropped everything to go for a trial in Italy. That was the making of Australia's next star.

item.title: ASX closes lower but gains 2.8pc over financial year — as it happened
item.description: The Australian share market traded mostly flat on Tuesday before a late afternoon slump. On Wall Street, the Dow Jones Industrial Average hit a record high with futures pointing to more positive trade ahead.

item.title: Retiring Ben Stokes backs Harry Brook as England's next Test captain
item.description: Ben Stokes says vice-captain Harry Brook has his "100 per cent" support to lead England's Test side, but coach Brendon McCullum is less effusive.

item.title: The controversial $1m Fitzroy River plan is closing for consultation. What is it?
item.description: Public submissions for the million-dollar draft Fitzroy-Derby water management plan close today. Here's everything you need to know.

item.title: ACCC sues Amazon over alleged unfair conduct for introducing Prime streaming service ads
item.description: The consumer watchdog is suing Amazon over alleged unfair contract terms for its Prime streaming service.

item.title: Police search for author of threatening letters sent to politicians, religious organisations over 10-year span
item.description: Police have asked for the community's assistance to identify the author targeting parliamentarians and religious organisations, who goes by 'Scorpio' or 'Bullit'.

item.title: Ernst and Young staff sacked after Anthony Albanese's private banking information allegedly breached
item.description: Two Ernst and Young employees have been sacked after Prime Minister Anthony Albanese's personal banking information was allegedly breached while the pair worked for the accounting firm at the Commonwealth Bank.

item.title: 'Kick in the guts': Injured NT veterans join calls for removal of $5,000 health funding cap
item.description: The federal government wants to cap access to physical and mental health supports to $5,000 a year for injured war veterans, but there are growing concerns the move will push some into "a worse-off situation".

item.title: TPG Capital private equity boss Joel Thickins pleads guilty to drink-driving-related offences
item.description: Private equity baron Joel Thickins has had his license suspended after pleading guilty to negligent driving while under the influence of alcohol and refusing a breath test.

item.title: Yancoal's Ashton coal mine to close in 2028
item.description: Hundreds of jobs will be lost over the next 18 months as a major coal operation in the New South Wales Upper Hunter begins a staged closure.

item.title: World Cup: Lifeless Germany fall as Paraguay show tenacity to die another day
item.description: Paraguay's upset penalty-shootout win over Germany at the World Cup, while stunning, was not entirely unexpected, as they showed impressive determination to die another day.

item.title: 'MrBeast model': Is Stefanovic chasing the far right or the algorithm?
item.description: Why is the former king of Australian breakfast television trading studio lights for ring lights?

item.title: Don't ask me what's culturally appropriate, I don't know either
item.description: After sending out invitations to a belated elopement party, I was suddenly fielding calls from friends who wanted to wear saris but were worried that it might be disrespectful.

item.title: Watchdog extends prohibition on Emily Lal providing freebirth services
item.description: Emily Lal, who previously operated online as The Authentic Birthkeeper, will be banned from advertising, providing advice and health services for another three months while the Victorian health watchdog investigates.

item.title: Federal MP Josh Burns slams social media giants for antisemitism response at royal commission
item.description: Federal Victorian MP Josh Burns details an attack on his electorate office where a hammer was used to smash the windows, and the following abuse on social media.

item.title: Australian suspect in Thailand teen murder case went about 'normal life', police allege
item.description: Australian Simon Peter Carman "lived his normal life", going out, washing his clothes and talking to neighbours after allegedly murdering a teenage girl and stuffing her body inside a suitcase in Thailand, police say.

item.title: World Cup quick hits: Penalty shootout, VAR controversy make for German nightmare
item.description: The knockout stages of the World Cup are in full swing, with penalty shootout drama and VAR controversy already featuring. Here are today's quick hits.

item.title: Nervous wait for snow in NSW alps ahead of school holidays
item.description: Snow may finally be on the way as NSW ski resorts ride out a slow start to the season.

item.title: Suspected saltwater crocodile takes dog at Flinders River near Richmond
item.description: Richmond Shire Mayor John Wharton believes a dog has been taken by a saltwater crocodile in the Flinders River, hundreds of kilometres inland from its usual habitat.

item.title: Temporary ban to protect Whyalla's 'significantly low' cuttlefish numbers
item.description: From Wednesday, a 90-day exclusion will begin at the cuttlefish sanctuary zone between Black Point and Stony Point in South Australia's Upper Spencer Gulf.

item.title: Liberal Party set to dump Moira Deeming as candidate after assault allegation
item.description: Controversial MP Moira Deeming is set to be deselected as a Liberal Party candidate for the November state election for refusing to apologise to partyroom colleague Matthew Guy.

item.title: Fossil kept in drawer for 40 years is Antarctica’s first dinosaur bone
item.description: A bone found in Antarctica in 1985, and kept in a drawer in England for almost four decades, turns out to belong to one of the largest types of dinosaurs to ever walk the Earth.

item.title: Ahead of his Origin recall, can Bradman Best be New South Wales's saviour again?
item.description: In just two matches Bradman Best has earned a reputation as a State of Origin saviour and New South Wales will be desperate for a repeat of those heroics in the decider.

item.title: Five tonnes of antibiotic florfenicol used last summer to treat disease, Tasmanian salmon industry says
item.description: More than five tonnes of antibiotics were used to treat a disease that killed 9,000 tonnes of fish last summer, Salmon Tasmania says.

item.title: SA coroner finds 'no genuine opportunity' to prevent double-fatal Urrbrae crash
item.description: A South Australian coroner says it is an "unfortunate truth" that there was no way for police to predict or prevent a collision that killed a senior police officer and another woman in 2020.

item.title: Two deals show Donald Trump's different approaches to key players in Middle East conflicts
item.description: Two recent Middle East deals, one in Iran and the other in Lebanon, seem at odds with each other, and show how the Trump administration is running different approaches depending on who it is talking to at any given moment.

item.title: Rex Airlines breached continuous disclosure obligations, court finds
item.description: Rex contravened its continuous disclosure obligations, misleading the market, the NSW Supreme Court found on Tuesday.

item.title: Perth paediatrician Chia Liang Saw accused of child sex offences now facing 53 charges
item.description: A Perth paediatrician accused of using hidden cameras to record himself abusing children is now facing more than 50 charges involving 10 alleged victims, with a court told further charges are likely to be laid.

item.title: Whyalla steelworks jobs at risk if 'one last crack' to restart blast furnace fails
item.description: The aging blast furnace at the Whyalla steelworks could be permanently shut down before the steelworks transitions to a direct-reduced iron plant and electric arc furnace, placing hundreds of jobs at risk.

item.title: Sunraysia Football Netball League adopts new concussion app to improve transparency
item.description: A sporting league in regional Victoria is the first to adopt a concussion app aimed at improving communication and accountability around concussions at grassroots sports.

item.title: Australian horticulture hits record production value of $19.5 billion
item.description: The value of Australia's horticulture sector has more than doubled in a decade, according to data released today by the Australian Bureau of Statistics.

item.title: Over 200 artefacts from more than 100 years of Antarctic exploration on display at National Museum of Australia
item.description: For most, a trip to Antarctica is well beyond reach, but a new exhibition at the National Museum of Australia gives visitors the chance to explore life in one of the most extreme environments on Earth.

item.title: Thunderbirds co-captain to lead 'connected' team for Super Netball Grand Final
item.description: The Adelaide Thunderbirds are looking to win their third premiership in four years, with co-captain Georgie Horjus putting their success down to strong team connections on and off the court.

item.title: TGA warns several sexual enhancement supplements contain hidden prescription drugs
item.description: The TGA issues a warning for consumers using sexual enhancement products after an investigation finds several products contain undeclared prescription drugs that could cause serious side effects.

item.title: Anger in Far North Queensland after Hill electorate abolished in redistribution
item.description: There is widespread anger among voters of an abolished Queensland electorate who will potentially face an 11-hour drive to their local MP's office.

item.title: CSIRO modelling explores flood-mitigation options for Richmond Valley
item.description: CSIRO modelling shows even the construction of a series of upstream "detentions" would not have spared Lismore from the 2022 floods.

item.title: Melbourne childcare provider stripped of federal funding over serious safety breaches
item.description: The Melbourne childcare provider has been cut off under new powers that allow the federal government to withdraw funding due to safety breaches.

item.title: What critics are saying about Legally Blonde prequel Elle
item.description: Legally Blonde was an overwhelming hit, so what do critics think of the prequel Elle?

item.title: Menswear brand accused of using AI to 'whitewash' model’s image
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Australian model accuses menswear brand Peter Jackson of using AI to 'whitewash' his image
item.description: Australian model Elii Emeghebo is pursuing a racial discrimination complaint against suit label Peter Jackson, claiming AI has been used to "whitewash" his image.

item.title: Scientists baffled at 60 per cent drop in migrating whale sightings off South West of WA
item.description: Marine scientists have expressed concerns following a 60 per cent drop in migrating whales seen off South West WA's coastline compared to previous years.

item.title: Two fined, banned from keeping dogs for 5 years after malnourished animal seized
item.description: Two people have been convicted and disqualified from owning dogs for five years after a severely emaciated dog was rescued by RSPCA inspectors and nursed back to health.

item.title: Basketball coach Shannon Trew abused girl during school training session, court told
item.description: Shannon Trew, 42, indecently dealt with a young girl he was training at a primary school, a Perth court hears.

item.title: His high school teacher asked him for money to gamble, now he’s fighting for an advertising crackdown
item.description: When Liberal backbencher Simon Kennedy was a teenager, one of his high school teachers asked to borrow money for his gambling addiction. He says he ended up accompanying the teacher to Gamblers Anonymous meetings.

item.title: NT residents are the nation's happiest, university study finds
item.description: Northern Territory residents are the happiest in the nation, a new university study has found, with access to nature, warm weather and cultural diversity among the theories for Territorians' sunny attitudes.

item.title: Usman Khawaja helps launch Islamophobia awareness campaign as anti-Muslim rhetoric ramps up across Australia
item.description: The retired 39-year-old, who was the first Muslim to play international cricket for Australia, says Islamophobia is on the rise, with women most vulnerable to attacks, because "hate is more prevalent that it has been before".

item.title: Tuesday finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: June sees biggest monthly fall in national housing values since 2022, Cotality finds
item.description: Australian home values record their biggest decline in more than three years as the housing market slowdown that began in Sydney and Melbourne spreads across the country.

item.title: US Supreme Court rejects Donald Trump's bid to restrict birthright citizenship
item.description: Donald Trump's controversial attempt to restrict birthright citizenship in the US is rejected by the Supreme Court, up-ending the president's hardline agenda on immigration.

item.title: Live: Wild weather in Mexico delays game, France's fantastic four dismantles Sweden
item.description: Kylian Mbappé scores twice for France as it tears Sweden apart, while Erling Haaland nets an 88th-minute winner to send Norway through and eliminate Côte d'Ivoire. Follow live.

item.title: How to protect your child from head knocks in sport
item.description: When your child decides they are keen on playing sport, there are lots of factors to consider as a parent, and after recent news stories about chronic traumatic encephalopathy, keeping them safe from head injuries may be front of mind.

item.title: In wake of KPMG scandal, government considers splitting accounting firms' auditing and consulting arms
item.description: Accounting firms could be asked to split their lucrative consulting services from their audit functions and individual firm partners could face far greater scrutiny and penalties for legal breaches, under a shake up of the industry being consulted on by the federal government.

item.title: Small NDIS providers and families on edge as registration 'cyclone' looms
item.description: Uncertainty and confusion are sweeping across the NDIS provider sector with mandatory registration for two categories of providers starting today.

item.title: July 1 brings new rules, a tax cut and an increase to paid parental leave
item.description: A bunch of new rules come into effect today. Find out how they could impact your finances.

item.title: Photos reveal room where Australian man allegedly murdered Thai teenager
item.description: New photographs reveal the cluttered room where 17-year-old Tunchanok Donhomla was allegedly murdered by Australian man Simon Peter Carman.

item.title: Brisbane 2032 is six years away, but the pressure has already arrived
item.description: Australia’s brightest young sporting talents are being tipped for Brisbane 2032, but experts warn it may be too soon.

item.title: Travel quiz, July 1: Do you have what it takes today?
item.description: Where would you find the iconic Forrest Gump bench? And do you know where this bridge is?

item.title: Coalition focuses fire on Labor, not One Nation, in daily talking points
item.description: Coalition talking points, obtained by the ABC, make almost no direct mention of One Nation despite the minor party surging in the polls.

item.title: From talking sheep to incel horror, here are the best movies of 2026 so far
item.description: It's officially halfway through 2026 and the year so far has brought cinematic delights as well as a fair share of silver-screen stinkers. Here are our critics' top picks.

item.title: The condition that causes people to get lost in their own home
item.description: Some estimates suggest up to one in 30 people may be affected by a condition that has been described as a lifelong inability to orient oneself — even in extremely familiar surroundings.

item.title: World Cup: Ajdin Hrustic says 'ask the boss' about Socceroos penalty preparations
item.description: The Socceroos know their knockout game against Egypt could come down to penalties, but they aren't revealing anything about their strategies.

item.title: Conversion practice ban legislation set to be debated in Tasmanian parliament
item.description: Tasmania was the last Australian jurisdiction to decriminalise homosexuality, but since then it has gone on to pass some of the country's most inclusive legislation. There's one final frontier — conversion practices.

item.title: Manhunt in Monaco after parcel bomb injures Ukrainian businessman
item.description: Police have declined to identify the presumed target of the blast, but several sources say it was Ukrainian-born businessman Vadym Yermolaiev, a permanent resident of Monaco who has acquired Cypriot nationality

item.title: Relief for home owners after Sunshine Coast properties spared from resumption
item.description: Dozens of homes on the Sunshine Coast have been spared after the Queensland government decided they were no longer required for a massive new public transport project.

item.title: Real-world incidents targeting Jews reinforce 'spiral of hate' online, royal commission hears
item.description: The royal commission has heard new research which found anti-Jewish hate increased online both after October 7, 2023 and the Bondi Beach terror attack in December.

item.title: Australia to play Poland in first Davis Cup tie held in Brisbane in almost a decade
item.description: The countries will face off in a 2026 World Group I tie at the Pat Rafter Arena on September 19 and 20.

item.title: Germany under threat from extremists and foreign powers, says security service BfV
item.description: Moscow-recruited saboteurs, Chinese spies, Iran-backed Islamist militants and violent neo-Nazis are growing problems, says intelligence service BfV.

item.title: Life-changing HIV medication made free in SA in step welcomed by advocates
item.description: When Kath Leane was diagnosed with HIV almost 40 years ago, she feared she would never see her children — let alone her grandchildren — grow up. But the antiretroviral treatment which helped make that possible is today being made free for all South Australians.

item.title: New research reveals Queensland's skin cancer burden, triggers calls for more prevention campaigns
item.description: Middle-aged Queenslanders undergo almost 1.5 million skin cancer procedures a year, according to one of the studies.

item.title: Beechworth doctors revert to home visits for patients after taxi service left town
item.description: When their local taxi service folded 18 months ago, Beechworth residents struggled to get to their medical appointments.

item.title: Victoria Police investigate second suspicious fire in as many days
item.description: A fire that engulfed a tobacco shop on Bridge Road in Richmond early this morning is being treated as suspicious.

item.title: NT teenagers say vapes are still commonplace and easily accessible despite crackdown
item.description: The ABC has spoken to a number of teenagers who say despite a crack down on vapes, they remain accessible and commonplace.

item.title: Serena Williams beaten by Maya Joint at Wimbledon in tennis great's return to grand slam
item.description: Australian Maya Joint holds her nerve to beat tennis legend Serena Williams in the 23-time grand slam champion's first professional tennis match in four years.

item.title: Australia hammers West Indies to claim a spot in the Women's T20 World Cup cricket final
item.description: Australia makes it through to another Women's T20 World Cup final, after a scary moment when West Indies star Deandra Dottin collapsed during the national anthems.

item.title: Social housing tenants unable to work more for fear of losing homes
item.description: Fremantle resident Trish Owen could lose the tenancy on her government-provided home if she works more hours — and a new study suggests many people are in the same situation.

item.title: LeBron James to leave Los Angeles Lakers after eight seasons
item.description: The 41-year-old, who helped the Lakers win the 2020 NBA title, is likely to continue to his career at another franchise, acknowledging his exit with a post on X.

item.title: Live: 'Alarming': PM questions why Ernst and Young grads had access to banking data
item.description: Anthony Albanese has questioned how two Ernst and Young graduates had access to personal banking data after one was charged with accessing his account. Follow live.

item.title: Alex de Minaur through to Wimbledon second round after tough day for Australian men
item.description: Alex de Minaur makes a strong start to Wimbledon in a first round that saw carnage for his Australian counterparts.

item.title: 'Hyperscale' data centre planned for remote Northern Territory's Murranji Station
item.description: The proposed 19,150-hectare 'Project Ares' would be built by Energy North on Murranji Station in the Barkly Region, as up to 12 proponents eye the NT for data centres.

item.title: Clermont community bands together for slow, steady flood recovery
item.description: Residents in the Central Queensland town of Clermont have been working through a backlog of home rebuilds and fencing six months on from the worst flooding the town has seen in 110 years.

item.title: Queensland farmers oppose proposed sandstone quarry on family farm
item.description: A young Queensland farmer says his family's property is under threat from a proposed sandstone quarry, but the company behind the project insists there is nothing to fear.

item.title: Fourteen children die as roof collapses at tutoring centre in Pakistan
item.description: Workers had been repairing tiles on a decrepit building in Lahore when the roof gave way and crushed the youngsters.

item.title: Live: ASX dips at open, while AI-buying frenzy drives Dow to record high
item.description: The Australian share market eases slightly in early trade, even as an AI stock-led rally boosted the Dow Jones to a record high for a second straight day. Follow live.

item.title: WA iconic Gloucester Tree reopens after safety upgrade
item.description: Tourism operators are celebrating the reopening of the southern WA landmark, the Gloucester Tree, with visitors making the 37-metre climb to its peak for the first time since its closure three years ago.

item.title: Desperate search through rubble as twin earthquakes leave Venezuela in ruins
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Venezuelan earthquake rescuers save three-year-old boy after six days underneath rubble
item.description: The death toll from Venezuela's devastating two earthquakes has risen to more than 1,900, as tens of thousands of people still remain unaccounted.

item.title: Victim-survivors of alleged sexual abuse speak out on legal impact of receiving EMDR therapy
item.description: Victim-survivors of alleged sexual abuse have spoken to the ABC of the tension between EMDR mental health therapy and seeking justice through the legal system.

item.title: National Trust of South Australia volunteers formally warned against speaking publicly
item.description: Volunteers who spoke to the media say they felt "threatened" with formal warnings from the National Trust of South Australia. They are now preparing to share their experiences in a parliamentary inquiry.

item.title: Heartbreaking loss prompts conversations about farmers' mental health in Talimba, NSW
item.description: Leanne Staniforth says her husband, John, suffered in silence before his death by suicide. She is raising her voice to break down the stigma around depression.

item.title: ACCC uses new powers to knock back proposed Coles supermarket in Kalgoorlie, WA
item.description: In an unprecedented move, Australia's consumer watchdog has exercised new powers to block the opening of a Coles supermarket, on the grounds it could knock out smaller competitors in the surrounding region.

item.title: Power, water and sewerage prices across the NT increase by 5.3 per cent from today
item.description: The Northern Territory treasurer has defended an increase in power, water and sewerage prices, which are set to rise by 5.3 per cent from today.

item.title: BHP, Rio Tinto launch two battery-electric haulers but 62 diesel-powered trucks wait in wings
item.description: BHP and Rio Tinto have put their two battery-electric trucks on show, but is an aversion to risk stifling meaningful change?

item.title: New laws for WA tow truck operators come into effect today
item.description: The new laws come into effect from today, including "fit and proper person" tests for business operators.

item.title: 'Recurring issues' in helicopter sector following surveillance across Northern Australia
item.description: Australia's aviation watchdog has warned that cutting corners on maintenance and certification requirements could create a "real safety risk".

item.title: Victorian Liberals can't keep on track even with a prize in sight
item.description: The Liberals, it seems, can't resist returning to damaging infighting even in the one part of the country where things have been going quite encouragingly for them.

item.title: Rural disability advocate wheels 440km to raise money for mental health support
item.description: Sam Longmore is wheeling across the Riverina in the rain to raise money for mental health services.

item.title: Maya Joint says walking with Serena Williams onto Wimbledon's centre court her 'craziest moment'
item.description: Australian Maya Joint had to catch a glimpse of Serena Williams in the days leading up to their Wimbledon opener just to remove some of the mythos around the 23-time grand slam champion.

item.title: Melbourne childcare provider stripped of funding labels decision 'devastating'
item.description: A Victorian childcare provider stripped of its federal government funding over unresolved safety breaches has vowed to challenge the decision.

item.title: Alcoa to buy South32's alumina and bauxite assets for $8b
item.description: American mining giant Alcoa is set to buy $8 billion worth of assets from Australia's South32.

item.title: Canberra man pleads guilty to performing Nazi salutes in public and using white supremacist stickers
item.description: A Canberra man has pleaded guilty after performing Nazi salutes at two suburban shopping centres and placing white supremacist stickers over Indigenous and multicultural signs at the Australian National University.

item.title: World Cup: French unity on display against Sweden as their ruthless run to title rolls on
item.description: In a French team littered with superstars it is something intangible that could stop them taking another World Cup title, following another clinical display against Sweden in the round of 32.
